title: Red-Black Tree — Black Height Bound
description:
For a red-black tree with n nodes:
- Black height b satisfies: b ≤ h+1 ≤ 2b (where h is the tree height)
- Size satisfies: n ≥ 2^b - 1

Given n (number of nodes), compute:
1. The minimum possible black height b_min (smallest b with 2^b - 1 ≤ n)
2. The minimum possible height h_min = b_min - 1
3. The maximum possible height h_max = 2*b_max - 1 where b_max satisfies 2^b_max - 1 ≤ n

botCodes.code:
import math
n=int(input())
# b_min: smallest b with 2^b-1 <= n → b = floor(log2(n+1))
b_min=int(math.log2(n+1))
if 2**b_min-1>n:b_min-=1
b_min=max(1,b_min)
b_max=b_min  # same for upper bound on b
# h_min = b_min - 1 (all nodes black → perfect tree → height = b-1)
h_min=b_min-1
# h_max = 2*b_max - 1 (alternating red-black)
h_max=2*b_max-1
print(b_min,h_min,h_max)
let()=let n=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id in let b=ref 1 in while (1 lsl !b)-1<=n do incr b done;decr b;if !b<1 then b:=1;Printf.printf"%d %d %d\n" !b(!b-1)(2* !b-1)
